channel.title: SEAtoday - Learning & Education
channel.description: Upcoming events for SEAtoday - Learning & Education

item.title: April 2026 UW Department of Surgery Diversity Grand Rounds
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 6:30 AM

Location: University of Washington, Seattle, WA

Wednesday, Apr 1, 2026, 6:30 - 7:30 a.m. PDT LECTURE TITLE "TIME-SENSITIVE NERVE INJURIES: TRANSFORMING CARE THROUGHSURGERY, SCIENCE, AND SYSTEMS INNOVATION" SPEAKER YUSHA KATIE LIU, MD, PHD Assistant Professor of Surgery Director, Northwest Institute for Nerve Injury at Harborview Medical Center Division of Plastic Surgery University of Washington Department meeting and general information: dosadmin@uw.edu 206-543-3680 Campus location: Fialkow Biomedical Sciences Research Pavilion \(K wing\) \(HSK\) Campus room: K-069 Accessibility Contact: dosadmin@uw.edu / 206-543-3680 Event Types: Information Sessions, Lectures/Seminars

Event details

item.title: Community Police Commission Monthly Meeting
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Seattle City Hall, 600 4th Ave, Room 370, Seattle, WA

Venue: Seattle City Hall Address: 600 4th Ave Seattle, ns Neighborhoods: Citywide Audience: Adults Department: Boards and Commissions Website: http://www.seattle.gov/boards-and-commissions Contact: ocpc@seattle.gov Contact Email: ocpc@seattle.gov Pre-Register: No Link: https://www.seattle.gov/community-police-commission/meetings \*If you need accommodation for attendance, please follow the link below.\* www.seattle.gov… In-Person Seattle City Hall 600 4th Ave, Seattle, WA 98104 Room 370 Alterna tive Option \(Virtual\): Webinar topic: CPC Monthly Meeting Date and time: Wednesday, May 7, 2025 9:00 AM | \(UTC-07:00\) Paci fic Time \(US and Canada\) Join link: seattle.webex.com… Webinar number: 2494 697 6502 Webinar password: MMzWQMpM882 \(66997676 when dialing from a phone or video system\) Join by phone \+1-206-207-1700 United States Toll \(Seattle\) \+1-408-418-9388 United States Toll Access code: 249 469 76502

Event details

item.title: Free Tax Help
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 10 AM

Wednesday, April 1, 2026, 10am - 1:30pm Trained volunteers will be available to answer questions and prepare personal tax returns. Location Link: Broadview Branch Room Location: Meeting Room Registration Required: Registration is not required. Detailed Description: This service is free and available to individuals only. The free service is not available for business tax returns. To receive tax help, bring the following documents:, Required: Photo identification, Social Security cards or Individual Taxpayer Identification Numbers \(ITIN\) for everyone listed on the tax return., Proof of income, for example, W2s and 1099s \(Social Security benefits\), Official documents on Social Security Administration letterhead, Medicare card \(if Social Security number is followed by an "A"\), Be prepared to provide information about health care coverage for each person on the return., Recommended: Last year&'s tax return, if available, Bank account and routing numbers for direct deposit of refunds, Photocopies or computer print-outs of any of these documents willbe accepted., Due to federal budget cuts the IRS is unable to provide the Library with certain tax forms and instruction booklets., Most IRS forms are available online at www.irs.gov ., You may make free black-and-white prints of the forms from Library computers: www.spl.org/Print , Thanks to our partner AARP . Event Logistics: AARP Tax Help locations serve anyone who earned less than $80,000 in 2025., For eligibility details, contact AARP at 888-227-7669. Contact Us: If you have questions about this event, Ask Us or call 206-386-4636. ADA Accommodations: We can provide accommodations for people with disabilities at Library events. Please contactLEAP at least seven days before the event to request accommodations., All Library programs are free and open to the public.

Event details

item.title: Business Impact NW Drop Ins
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: 1st Wednesday, 10am-1pm (PST) 1/7/2026 2/4/2026 3/4/2026 4/1/2026 5/6/2026 6/3/2026 7/1/2026 8/5/2026 9/2/2026 10/7/2026 11/4/2026 12/2/2026, Kent, WA

Description Join us at the Kent Chamber of Commerce for free, in-person business support from Business Impact NW. Whether you?re launching a new venture or growing an existing one, drop in to get one-on-one guidance on business planning, funding options, certifications, and more. No appointment necessary?just bring your questions and your goals! Schedule time here - Calendly

item.title: April Membership Meeting: King County Aquatic Center Update
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 11:45 AM

Location: Federal Way Performing Arts and Event Center, 31510 Pete von Reichbauer Way S, Federal Way, WA

Join us for a keynote from Meagan Viken, Facility Supervisor of the King County Aquatic Center. Meagan manages the King County Aquatic Center, where she focuses on both high-level competition and community access. Under her leadership, the facility has hosted major events such as NCAA Division I Nationals, US Master&'s Summer Nationals, Washington State Special Olympics, while also expanding public programming-adding over 700 swimming lessons in the past year alone.

item.title: Renton Business Information Session: Renton Market, Pavilion & Piazza Updates
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 3 PM

Location: Renton Library, 100 Mill Ave S, Renton, WA

Join us for the next Renton Business Information Session on Tuesday, April 2 from 3 to 4 p.m. at the Renton Library, with a virtual option available. This session will feature updates on the Renton Market, Pavilion, and Piazza, with the Pavilion project team sharing what businesses can expect in the coming months and the latest information related to Legacy Square. These sessions are a great opportunity to stay informed, ask questions, and connect with the city and other members of the business community as downtown Renton continues to grow and evolve. All Renton businesses are welcome. April 2 \(first Thursday of the month\) ⏰ 3–4 p.m. Renton Library \(100 Mill Ave. S\) Virtual option available https://us06web.zoom.us/meeting/register/WV-tjbP-SHirbuMZmmDJtQ Learn more: rentonwa.gov/bizinfosessions

Event details

item.title: LANDSCAPE TECHNICIAN BOOTCAMP
item.description:
Friday, April 3, 2026 at 7 AM

Location: Lake Washington Institute of Technology - LWTech, 11605 132nd Ave NE, Kirkland, WA

LANDSCAPE TECHNICIAN BOOTCAMP Friday, April 3, 2026 Lake Washington Institute of Technology – 11605 132nd Avenue NE, Kirkland, WA 98034-8506 TITLE SPONSOR - DURANTA The Washington Association of Landscape Professionals is excited to offer the LANDSCAPE TECHNICIAN BOOTCAMP training in partnership with the National Association of Landscape Professionals. WALP will be offering the LANDSCAPE TECHNICIAN BOOTCAMP in APRIL 3, 2026 at Lake Washington Insitute of Technology that will be comprehensive training for your staff and workforce. Hands-On Training For Your Crews! The new Landscape Technician Boot Camp will provide hands-on training for your crews. Based on the curriculum for the Landscape Industry Certified Technician Exterior exam, this industry-vetted training will help prepare your crews to work more effectively in 2026. Boot Camp includes training in techniques and safety in 15 different competency areas organized into 3 disciplines presented in Spanish and English! Bonus! This course also helps prepare people for the Landscape Industry Certified Technician Exterior exam. So, once you’ve trained your team, consider registering them to become certified through the National Association of Landscape Professionals \(NALP\). Registration Costs: WALP Members \(First 5 registrants\): $95.00 per person WALP Members \(6\+ registrants\): $80.00 per person NON-WALP Members : $145.00 per person

Event details

Event details

item.title: Homebuyer Education Workshop
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: ULMS, 105 14th Avenue, Suite 200, Seattle, WA

This is a free five-hour workshop to help you meet the WA State Housing Finance Commission’s education requirements for down payment assistance and a variety of affordable housing programs and opportunities for qualified applicants! These requirements include the successful completion of a Homebuyers Education Seminar meeting the current FHA, Fannie Mae, FreddieMac, and SMBA benchmark standards.  At the conclusion of the workshop, participants will receive a certificate of achievement from the WA State Housing Finance Commission and DFI valid for two years from the date of issuance.  To join our next workshop, please complete and submit the following registration form at least 24-hours before your selected workshop date. Once this form has been submitted, be sure to check your email for a digital confirmation, as well as additional follow up and important information from a ULMS Housing Department staff member.   In-person workshops are held every 1st and 2nd Saturday of the month from 10:00AM – 3:30PM. They will be held at our Main Office at the following address: 105 14th Ave, Suite 200 Seattle, WA 98122.

Event details

item.title: Sailing Shore School
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: CWB Boathouse, Lake Union, Seattle, WA

This 2-hour interactive course will introduce you to the basics of sailing. Topics will include terminology for parts of the boat and points of sail, as well as the basic theory of sailing. With the class you will receive the CWB Sailing Primer! Shore School can be taken independently or as part of the SailNOW! or US Sailing Basic Keelboat Certification courses. More information here

Event details

item.title: 3XBA Spring 3x3 Clinic Series
item.description:
Sunday, April 5, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: The Bush School, 3400 E Harrison St, Seattle, WA

Neighborhoods: Capitol Hill,Central Area/Squire Park,First Hill,Judkins Park,Madison Park,Madrona/Leschi,Montlake/Portage BayEvent Types: Community,Education,Hobbies,Holiday/Seasonal Events, Sports/Recreation,Youth Audience: Children,Special Needs,Teen S ponsoring Organization: 3x3 Basketball Association \(3XBA\) Contact: Lakin Roland Contact Email: info@3xba.com Pre-Register: Yes Cost: $250 \+ fees Registration Details: Two sessions offered each Sunday: Grades 2-5 from 9:00 AM to 11:00 AM, Grades 6-8 from 11:00 AM to 1:00 PM. The clinic runs for five Sundays: March 22, March 29, April 5, Apr il 26, and May 3, 2026 at The Bush School, Seattle. Early bird pricin g ends on March 1. Learn more about 3xBA and register at: https://www.3xba.com/register Link: https://3xba.ludus.com/index.php?show\_id=200517791 Help your athlete grow their game this spring with the 3xBA Spring 3x3 Clinic Series-a 5-week program combining fun, competit ive 3x3 basketball with small-group instruction led by 3xBA coaches. Players develop skills, confidence, and basketball IQ through structured play and hands-on coaching. Open to all experience levels for 2nd-8th graders. Athletes should bring a basketball and water bottle. Perfe ct for off-season development and team-style competition.

Event details

item.title: Forum Presents Family Easter Egg Hunt!

Sunday, April 5, 2026 at 11 AM

Hop into the Family Easter Egg Hunt at Forum Social House! Dance, face paint, photos with the Easter Bunny & enjoy our Bunny Brunch!

Event details

item.title: Spy Masters Academy
item.description:
Monday, April 6, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: Suncatcher Forest School, 18701 se 60th st, Issaquah, WA

Class Experience Calling all young secret agents! This time kids are welcome to join action-packed Outdoor Spy Camp, where kids will train to become top-notch spies through thrilling challenges, daring missions, and teamwork! Obstacle Courses & Ninja Training – Agents-in-training will test their agility, speed, and stealth as they navigate exciting ninja courses, crawl through the forest and learn to use nature objects to complete the challenge. Scavenger Hunts & Riddle Solving – Spies must sharpen their detective skills by decoding secret messages, solving riddles, and uncovering hidden clues to complete their top-secret missions. Nerf Battles & Tactical Challenges – Gear up for friendly Nerf wars, where teamwork and strategy are key to outsmarting the opposition. Agents will also practice their precision and accuracy with target practice and stealth maneuvers. Escape Room-Style Puzzles – Secret agents will work together to crack codes, unlock treasure chests, and solve mind-bending puzzles before time runs out! Outdoor Adventure & Camouflage Training – Spies will blend into their surroundings, practice silent movement, and use nature to their advantage while completing their missions. This high-energy camp is perfect for kids who love adventure, problem-solving, and action-packed fun in the great outdoors! Are you ready to accept the mission? - follow the ticket link to book the camp!

Event details

item.title: Capitol Hill & First Hill Business Safety Summit
item.description:
Monday, April 6, 2026 at 3 PM

Location: Stoup Brewing, 1158 Broadway, Seattle, WA

Description You are invited to join fellow Capitol Hill and First Hill businesses for a Safety Summit at the Silver Cloud Hotel \(1100 Broadway\) on Mon, Apr 6 from 3:00 to 5:00 p.m. This gathering is designed specifically for businesses operating at the street level. This is a great opportunity to meet the new East Precinct Captain, hear about helpful programs from the City, and connect with neighboring businesses facing many of the same challenges. Our goal is to work together to support and strengthen what makes the business districts of Capitol Hill and First Hill so unique and special. This event is brought to you by the 15th Avenue Business Improvement Area, Broadway Business Improvement Area, First Hill Improvement Association, and GSBA: Capitol Hill, with additional support from 4N & Associates and Silver Cloud Hotel. Meet Our New East Precinct Captain Captain James Britt, SPD Community Assisted Response & Engagement \(CARE\) Chief Amy Barden, CARE Learn About Community Assisted Response & Engagement \(CARE\) Crime Prevention Through Environmental Design \(CPTED\) Back to Business Program: Storefront Repair Fund and Storefront Security Fund Share Bring your safety challenges, concerns and questions Program 3pm Doors open 3:30pm Speaking program starts followed by social Please RSVP Space is limited. Free Parking! Light food provided. Please bring cash for no host bar. Pre-event Survey Help shape the conversation at the Safety Summit by sharing any safety challenges, concerns, or questions you would like addressed. Please submit responses by Friday, April 3 so we can share common themes with presenters in advance. If you cannot attend, we still welcome your input and will share an event recap afterward.

Event details

item.title: Building a More Welcoming Washington: Seattle's FIFA World Cup Inclusion Training
item.description:
Monday, April 6, 2026 at 3 PM

Location: GSBA- Washington State&'s LGBTQ+ Chamber of Commerce, 400 E Pine St Ste 322, Seattle, WA

Description Inclusion Training Initiative: Building a More Welcoming Washington As Washington prepares to welcome the world for FIFA World Cup 2026, local businesses will be on the front lines of hospitality-serving visitors, supporting staff, and representing the values of our region every day. GSBA is partnering with SeattleFWC26 to offer Inclusion Training workshops designed to help small businesses and organizations feel prepared, confident, and welcoming. These sessions provide practical, real-world guidance on allyship, gender inclusion, and LGBTQ\+ cultural competency, with a focus on everyday customer and workplace interactions. About the Inclusion Training Initiative As part of SeattleFWC26's commitment to ensuring every fan, visitor, and resident feels welcome, the Inclusion Training Initiative-delivered in partnership with GSBA-equips local businesses and organizations with tools to create authentically inclusive environments for LGBTQ\+ customers, employees, and communities. About River Teslar River \(she/her\) is a trans woman and nonprofit professional with four years of experience at GSBA developing and facilitating LGBTQ\+ Inclusion Trainings through a social justice- and trauma-informed lens. Drawing from lived experience and community-based work, she creates learning spaces grounded in empathy, curiosity, and practical application, where participants feel supported engaging at any level. Key Activities ⚽ Four Inclusion Workshops: Hosted by GSBA and tailored for small businesses, nonprofits, and frontline teams. ⚽ Interactive Learning: Sessions focus on allyship, gender inclusion, and LGBTQ\+ cultural competency in the workplace, with real-world scenarios and discussion. ⚽ Accessible Participation: Two in-person workshops hosted at GSBA's Capitol Hill office \(or other community venues\) and two virtual workshops open to participants statewide. ⚽ Practical Tools: Participants receive actionable guidance and resources to help strengthen inclusive policies, communications, and customer experiences. Outcomes & Benefits ⚽ More Inclusive Businesses: Participating organizations gain the knowledge to create spaces that welcome all genders, identities, and relationships. ⚽ Community Visibility: Strengthens Washington's reputation as a leader in inclusive tourism and community hospitality ahead of FIFA World Cup 2026. ⚽ Economic Impact: Inclusive environments encourage repeat visitation, word-of-mouth referrals, and stronger customer loyalty. ⚽ Sustained Legacy: Builds long-term capacity for inclusion that extends beyond the tournament, leaving a lasting mark on Washington's business and tourism ecosystem. Workshop Schedule Virtual Workshops ⚽ Thursday, January 15 - 10:00 AM ⚽ Wednesday, March 4 - 3:00 PM In-Person Workshops ⚽ Monday, February 9 - 10:00 AM ⚽ Monday, April 6 - 3:00 PM Learn more about the initiative: SeattleFWC26 Inclusion Training Initiative

Event details

Location: Skyline High School, 1122 228th Ave SE, Sammamish, WA

Come join the Booster Club meeting in the Skyline Library to get to know other booster parents and see how you can get involved. Not sure what Boosters is or how to support? This is another great way to get involved or find out more. We can't wait to see you!

Event details

item.title: EMBODIED ALTARS DANCE CLASS
item.description:
Monday, April 6, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way, Seattle, WA

EMBODIED ALTARS is an all-levels dance class hosted by Daybreak Star Radio. Designed by Indigenous Chicana artist and El Sueño director, Alicia Mullikin. Dancers will utilize Indigenous principles within movement, meditation, and song as a way to practice gratitude, honor the land, and recognize their ancestors. The class is for those who are interested in reflection, intention setting, and a discovery of their own stories. No dance experience necessary. Class Time: Mondays, 6:30-8PM No class 9/15/25 No class 10/13/25 Venue: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way, Seattle, WA 98199 Sliding Scale: $10-$20\* per class. Sign Up Now!   This venue is ADA accessible w/ ADA bathrooms and a wheelchair lift to the second floor. Class will be held in the ballroom space, this area has 5 ADA parking spaces. There are an additional 25 parking spots in our lot, there is ample parking space along the road leading up to Daybreak star as well as the Discovery Park North Parking lot. What to bring: water, comfortable clothes to dance in, journal, your personal identity, a desire to explore new pathways and an open mind/heart. Hosted at Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center Produced by United Indians of All Tribes Foundation In Partnership with El Sueno Dance & Daybreak Star Radio “Indigenize the Airwaves” “Art. Culture. Reclamation.” \*Talk to us about BIPOC and Queer/LGBTQIA2S\+ Scholorships\* Please contact @el.sueno.dance on IG for private conversations about this topic.

Event details

item.title: Executive Council (EC) Meeting
item.description:
Tuesday, April 7, 2026 at 8:45 AM

Location: University of Washington - Bothel, 18115 Campus Way NE, Bothell, WA

Location Type: Online Event sponsors: UW Bothell General Faculty Organization Accessibility Contact: dawn3@uw.edu If you would like to attend, please email Dawn Moncalieri, GFO Progr am Coordinator, at dawn3@uw.edu to request the zoom link. \*\*\*AN AGENDA WILL BE ADDED HERE ONE WEEK PRIOR TO MEETING\*\*\* The Executive Council is an elected faculty council of the GFO. Its responsibilities are to advise the Chance llor and Vice Chancellors on matters involving academic policy, policy regarding faculty promotion and tenure; and the development and implementation of the campus vision statement, and on the general welfare of the campus. EC meetings occur every other Tuesday, 8:45-10 :45am. To learn more about the EC, see the full 2024/25 meeting s chedule, and view council membership, CLICK HERE .

Event details

Microsoft business applications innovation, education, and training day bringing the Summit NA experience directly to Dynamics & D365 users.

A workshop about Ancestral rest, Movement, and pelvic floor care for birthworkers.

Event details

item.title: Digital Humanities: Intersections and Challenges
item.description:
Tuesday, April 7, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: Advancement & Alumni Building, Stuart T. Rolfe Room, Seattle, WA

What Is Digital Humanities—and Why Does It Matter Now? Digital humanities is an interdisciplinary field that combines humanistic inquiry with digital tools to transform how we study culture, language, history, and society. From digital archives and multilingual publishing to data-driven cultural analysis, the field is reshaping research, teaching, and public scholarship worldwide. The Roundglass India Center & Technology Ethics Initiative invite students and faculty to explore this rapidly evolving space through a special lecture by Professor Nirmala Menon, an internationally recognized leader in digital humanities. Professor Menon is Chair Professor at the School of Humanities and Social Sciences at the Indian Institute of Technology Indore, where she leads the Digital Humanities and Publishing Research Group and is Chair of the JPN National Centre of Excellence in the Humanities. She also serves as an Affiliate Research Professor at the University of Oxford and is currently a Fulbright Fellow at Northeastern University \(2025–26\). Her scholarship spans postcolonial studies, digital humanities, and multilingual knowledge systems, with a sustained focus on decolonizing global knowledge infrastructures. Dr. Menon's Talk: Digital Knowledge Infrastructures and Digital Humanities Projects: Intersections and Challenges In this timely talk, Professor Menon examines the uneven and evolving trajectory of digital humanities \(DH\) in India and the broader research ecosystem. She explores the skepticism and resistance the field sometimes encounters—including concerns about distancing from core humanities questions or borrowing the rhetoric of STEM for legitimacy—while making the case that DH is far more than its critiques. Professor Menon argues that humanities scholars must actively harness, interrogate, and shape digital technologies to ensure that enduring humanistic questions remain central to technological development and deployment. Drawing on major initiatives—including Project KSHIP at IIT Indore, the DST-sponsored SINDHU project, the Electronic Literature from India anthology, and the work of Open Access India—she highlights efforts to decolonize knowledge systems, expand multilingual scholarship, and make research more accessible and equitable. The talk will also address the continuing structural challenges facing humanities research infrastructures and invite conversation about the future of digital scholarship in global and Indian contexts. Students, faculty, and researchers interested in digital scholarship, global humanities, publishing, technology and society, and South Asia are especially encouraged to attend.

Event details

item.title: 1st
item.description:
Tuesday, April 7, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: SCERA, 625 S. 4th Street, Renton, WA

MAXIMIZE YOUR MEMBERSHIP Congratulations, you have joined the Chamber of Commerce! Now what? Here is where you learn to use the SUPER 6 Tools, when you log in to your MIC \(Member Information Center\). Maximize Your Membership 101 workshop is designed to educate our valued members on the benefits of using your Chamber Master access. We will also cover networking opportunities and advocacy that are available to you as a member of the Renton Chamber of Commerce. This training session will equip you with the ability to navigate and utilize your MIC Tools to effectively promote your business reaching over 10k potential customers. Posting Jobs, Events, Member 2 Member and Hot Deals, and more, will be shown. MYM101 is an essential to for new members onboarding and current members who need a refresher course, who want MAXimize Your Membership! Our membership is similar to a gym... the more you use it, the better the results! We also welcome businesses who are interested in chamber membership to join us! FREE!

Event details

item.title: Entrepreneurial Mash Up
item.description:
Tuesday, April 7, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Location: Columbia Tower Club, 701 5th Ave, 75th Flr, Seattle, WA

Description Entrepreneurial Mash Up The city's doers, dreamers, and makers in one place. Date: Tuesday, April 7 Time: 5:30-7:30 PM Location: Columbia Tower Club Price: $35 Come meet the communities shaping Seattle's entrepreneurial soul. Columbia Tower Club, GSBA, and Entrepreneurs' Organization are coming together to host an evening built for connection, curiosity, and shared momentum. This is your chance to learn what each organization brings to the region's business landscape and meet the people who make those missions real. Expect conversations that open doors, introductions that expand your world, and a room filled with good energy and big ideas. Show up ready to connect. Leave with a wider circle and a stronger network. This event is for active GSBA members only - you must be logged in to your InfoHub to purchase tickets. Due to extremely limited capacity, each organization has access to 35 tickets. About the Hosts About GSBA GSBA is Washington State's LGBTQ\+ and allied chamber of commerce. GSBA invests in economic vitality, business development, and inclusive leadership through programming, advocacy, and the GSBA Scholarship & Education Fund. About EO Seattle EO Seattle is the local chapter of the Entrepreneurs' Organization, a global network of founders and business owners committed to learning, growth, and community. Members connect through peer learning, events, and leadership development opportunities. About Columbia Tower Club Located at the top of Columbia Center, Columbia Tower Club is Seattle's premier private business and social club. The Club convenes leaders from across industries for dining, professional connection, and memorable experiences.

Event details

item.title: Type Design Basics
item.description:
Tuesday, April 7, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Seattle Central College Continuing Education, 1701 Broadway, Seattle, WA

Class Overview So you want to design a typeface? Learn how to hand-draw letters and digitize them to create your own unique functional font! In this practical workshop, students will create an original text typeface based on traditional European calligraphy. Whether you want to develop your typography skills, improve your lettering practice, or become a type designer, learning the fundamentals of type design can bring your skills to the next level. Explore both analog media \(pen on paper\) and digital media \(computer\). Learn how to use a calligraphy pen to develop an understanding of the underlying structure of the Latin alphabet. Then you can work on improving your drawings digitally, and create a typeface using Glyphs \(or the font editor of your choice\).   Class time is dedicated to working on your project with individualized feedback, supplemented by lectures, technical demonstrations, and group critique. By the end of the class, you will have a working text typeface of your own creation, as well as a sketch-to-screen workflow that you can use for future font creation. Students will be encouraged to spend up to 10 hours on homework outside of class time. Designing a typeface is a laborious process, but time flies when experimenting, drawing, and editing! This class is offered in partnership with the amazing Seattle Creative Academy \(but this class is not part of their program and is not for credit/degree\)! See also: Experimental Lettering and other Graphic Design or art classes on our website. Learning Objectives Learn the basics of calligraphy Understand the structure and patterning of the Latin alphabet Create your own original letterforms Digitize and refine your original design Learn how to optimize a typeface for text Become familiar with a sketch-to-screen digital type design workflow Who Might Enjoy This Class? Anyone who wants to develop their own custom font Graphic designers, illustrators, lettering artists, type designers -- anyone with related experience who would like to hone their type design skills Architects, engineers, artists, crafters, writers, book lovers -- anyone interested in the form of letters who would enjoy creating and using their own handmade font No prerequisites, but basic graphic design/typography knowledge is recommended

Event details

item.title: Health and Wellness
item.description:
Tuesday, April 7, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Revitalize Spa, 8142 15th Ave SW, Seattle, WA

Description West Seattle Health & Wellness Professionals Meetup This is an evening of connection and collaboration with fellow health and wellness professionals in West Seattle! This is a fantastic opportunity to network, share insights, and build relationships within the local wellness community. Each attendee will have the chance to give a brief 1-2 minute introduction about their business, so come prepared to share what you do and how you serve the community. Cost: Free! Who?s Invited: Any health and wellness professional looking to expand their network Register now and take advantage of this opportunity to connect with like-minded professionals!

Event details

item.title: Tech Tuesdays at a/stir on Capitol Hill
item.description:
Tuesday, April 7, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Capitol Cider, 818 E Pike St, Seattle, WA

Craving real face time after too many Zooms? Come hang out at a/stir on Broadway for an easygoing tech-community mixer.

Event details

item.title: Livestream-Public Employees Retirement System (PERS) Retirement Workshop
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: University of Washington, Seattle, WA

Online Wednesday, Apr 8, 2026, 9 a.m. - 12 p.m. PDT Who should attend: Employees currently participating in a PERS, TRS, SERS or LEOFF Retirement Plan and who expect to retire within the next 12 months. Please contact the Department of Retirement Systems \(DRS\) at to request your retirement estimate and application or visit www.drs.wa.gov . This 3-hour workshop is designed for those retiring within the year and covers the following topics: Retirement eligibility Completing the DRS and UW retirement applications Retiree medical plan choices and enrollment Applying for Medicare Leave balances and VEBA, if applicable Notifying your department about your separation Working after retirement Campus room: Benefits Conference Center Accessibility Contact: benefits@uw.edu Event Types: Workshops Event sponsors: UW Human Resources Benefits Office More info: ucs.admin.uw.edu…

Event details

item.title: Campus Council on Planning and Budget (CCPB) Meeting
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: University of Washington - Bothel, 18115 Campus Way NE, Bothell, WA

Location Type: Online Event sponsors: UW Bothell General Faculty Organization Accessibility Contact: dawn3@uw.edu If you would like to attend, please email Dawn Moncalieri, GFO Progr am Coordinator, at dawn3@uw.edu to request the zoom link. \*\*\*AN AGENDA WILL BE ADDED HERE ONE WEEK PRIOR TO MEETING\*\*\* The UW Bothell Campus Council on Planning and Budgeting \(CCPB\) serves as an advisory board to the UW Bothell Executive Council on long-range strategic planning, preparation of budgets, a nd distribution of funds as they relate to issues of concern to faculty. This council meets monthly. To learn more about CC PB, see the full meeting schedule, and view membership CLICK HERE.

Event details

item.title: Chamber 101
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 3:30 PM

Location: Chamber Office, 3100 S 176th St, SeaTac, WA

Chamber 101: An Introduction to Your Local Chamber of Commerce Chamber 101 is an informative and engaging session designed to help members, prospective members, and community partners understand what the Chamber of Commerce is, how it operates, and how it supports local businesses. During this event, attendees will learn: What a Chamber of Commerce is and the role it plays in strengthening the local business ecosystem. What the Chamber does, including advocacy, networking, business development, community partnerships, and economic growth initiatives. How membership works, what benefits are available, and how to make the most of the resources, programs, and visibility opportunities offered by the Chamber. Ways to get involved, from attending events and committees to leveraging promotional tools and support services. Chamber 101 is ideal for anyone looking to better understand how the Chamber supports business success and how to fully engage with its mission and network. This session provides a welcoming space to ask questions, meet Chamber staff, and connect with fellow community members.

Event details

item.title: Friends of Lake Sammamish State Park Lakeside Lecture Bats Bats Bats
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: Lake Sammamish State Park, 2010 Northwest Sammamish Road, Issaquah, WA

Friends of Lake Sammamish State Park, welcome Bats Northwest Board member and biologist Bernice Tannebaum for a Lakeside Lecture about Bats of Northwest, bat boxes and luckily for anyone who attends this event, the incredible depth of Bernice bat knowledge. She was featured this summer in the Seattle Times. The event is a free indoor event but a donation gift is incurabed to help continue the great programming of the Friends of Lake Sammamish. https://wl.donorperfect.net/weblink/weblink.aspx?name=E362706&id=103

Event details

item.title: SP 101: Intro to Vera's Screen Print Studio
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Book-It Repertory Theatre, 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, WA

This 2-hour, comprehensive hands-on class teaches you all the basics of screen printing at VERA’s screen printing studio: From hand-drawing a stencil on transparency, coating and burning a screen, to pulling your very first print! Get comfortable with Vera's shop layout, equipment, and culture. Learn techniques for creating screens, terminology, and more to create 1-color projects in the print shop. Students will walk away with the basic skills for screen printing 1-color paper prints, and their own print of a special Vera design, specific to the 101 class. This class is a prerequisite for 200-level classes at VERA, as well as access to Open Sessions to work on your own projects in VERA’s screen printing studio.

Event details

item.title: Parenting Mercer Island PARENT LAB
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 6 PM

PARENT LABS are in-person interactive workshops for Mercer Isla nd parents/caregivers of K-12 students. Visit www.mihealthyyouth.com /parent-lab-2025-26 for details and registration. WINTER/SPRI NG 2026 SCHEDULE LAB \#4 - Just for DADS Elementary \(Grades K-5\) - January 13, 2026, 6:00-7:30 PM @ Lakeridge Elementary School Library. Middle/High School \(Grades 6-12\) - January 22, 2026, 6:30-8:00 PM @ Mercer Island High School \(MIHS\) Library. LAB \#5 - Supporting a Neurodiv erse Child Elementary \(Grades K-5\) - March 17, 2026, 6:00-7:30 PM @ Lakeridge Elementary School Library. Middle/High School \(Grades 6-12\) - Ma rch 12, 2026, 6:30-8:00 PM @ Mercer Island High School \(MIHS\) Library. LAB \#6 - Parenting Between Two Cultures - for Immigrant Parents Thi s Parent Lab is for parents of children of all grades \(K-12\) April 8, 2026, 6:00-7:30 PM @ Lakeridge Elementary School Library Questions? Email healthyyouth@mercerisland.gov Opens in new window PDF Download Word Download Excel Download PowerPoint Download Document Download Opens in new window PDF Download Word Download Exc el Download PowerPoint Download Document Download Opens in new window PDF Download Word Download Excel Down load PowerPoint Download Document Download Opens in new window PDF Download Word Download Excel Download PowerPoint Download Document Download Opens in new window PDF Download Word Download Excel Download PowerPoi nt Download Document Download

item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: University of Washington, Seattle, WA

This 3 hour workshop is designed for those retiring within the year and covers the following topics: Retirement eligibility Completing the UW retirement application Retiree medical plan choices and enrollment Applying for Medicare Leave balances and VEBA, if applicable Notifying your department about your separation Working after retirement Accessibility Contact: benefits@uw.edu Event Types: Lectures/Seminars Event sponsors: UW Human Resources Benefits office More info: ucs.admin.uw.edu…

item.title: GSBA Capitol Hill Monthly Safety Call
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 10 AM

Description GSBA: Capitol Hill Safety Call Join GSBA, Jen Carl, and Gabriel Neuman for our monthly GSBA: Capitol Hill Safety Call. Held on Zoom, these virtual conversations provide space for neighborhood updates, safety resources, and open dialogue with local businesses and community partners. We meet on the second Thursday of every month at 10am-all are welcome to join the discussion. Zoom link sent after registering and you can add this recurring meeting to your calendar below: ADD TO GOOGLE CALENDAR ADD TO OUTLOOK CALENDAR Need a link to Jen&'s safety resources? Click here. About GSBA: Capitol Hill GSBA: Capitol Hill provides business development, engagement, and advocacy for Capitol Hill businesses and nonprofits who are committed to our collective economic vitality.

item.title: Renton Chamber of Commerce Drop-In Business Coaching
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: SCERA, 625 S. 4th Street, Renton, WA

Are you thinking of starting a business? Do you already have a business but want it to grow? Are you facing a business puzzle that you’d like help with? Schedule your free 30-minute session with one of our talented business coaches! https://businessimpactnw.org/series/renton-chamber-of-commerce-drop-in-business-coaching-2025/ Drop-In Coaching sessions are held at the Renton Chamber of Commerce, 625 S 4th St, Renton, WA 98057. Note: Please limit reservations to one session. FREE

Event details

item.title: Restoration Event | Native Plant Nursery
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 12 PM

First Thursdays in the Native Plant Nursery! Calling all n ative plant lovers! We're looking for volunteers to join us every first Th ursday of each month to help us care for the plants in our native plant nu rsery. Every year we host a couple thousand native plants in the nursery u ntil they're ready to be put in the ground in parks across the island. This will be a great opportunity to learn more about native plants, learn about basic plant care, and help us ensure our plants healthy before being put in their new homes. Tasks may include: weeding potted pla nts, up-potting or re-potting native plants, plant organizing, plant propagation, weeding gravel areas, tool care, nursery pot maintenance, a nd watering. When: Thursdays, 12pm-2pm \(join us on your lunch break!\) Where: Luther Burbank Park, Caretakers building What to bring: Dress for the weather in clothes you don't mind getting dirty \(think: long pants , boots or sneakers\). Bring water. Small snacks, gloves, and tools will be provided. Registration: https://volunteersignup.org/B4AFB

Event details

item.title: Contemporary Dance for Every Body
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: Seattle Central College Continuing Education, 1701 Broadway, Seattle, WA

Class Overview Would you like to cultivate body awareness through movement? Explore a grounded and natural approach to moving your body, led by a contemporary dance instructor. Through floorwork, movement sequences, and phrasing, this class cultivates body awareness. Explore efficiency in movement transitions. Develop a deeper understanding of how your body moves naturally in relation to the floor, emphasizing organic flow, continuity, spirality, softness, core engagement, and the intelligent use of gravity. Open your mind and body through guided movement! Explore points of support, releasing tension, and engaging the core to facilitate flow and prevent injury. Movement principles will draw from contemporary dance and somatic practices, encouraging dancers to move with clarity, sensitivity, and presence. This class is for dancers, actors, physical theater artists, yoga/movement practitioners, and anyone interested in exploring movement in their bodies -- no experience necessary. Designed to be inclusive, this class offers both accessibility and challenge to movers at various levels and all backgrounds.  Learning Objectives Understand and embody key principles of movement floorwork, including gravity, spirality, and core initiation. Improve movement continuity and flow between levels \(floor to standing and vice versa\). Explore softness and efficiency through core engagement. Identify and use anatomical points of support for safe and fluid movement. Develop new dance tools Feel more connected with the possibilities of your body in a conscious way to live a more present, healthy, and aware life. Who Might Enjoy This Class? Adult learners of all backgrounds \(18\+\), including dancers, actors, physical theater artists, yoga/movement practitioners Anyone interested in embodied movement practices. Some prior movement or dance experience is helpful but not required. The class will be structured to accommodate varying levels of experience and physical ability.

Event details

item.title: Norwegian Language Classes
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: National Nordic Museum, 2655 NW Market Street, Seattle, WA

This nine-week series will allow you to jump into the basics of Norwegian language and culture in a supportive, face-to-face classroom environment. Students will learn essential vocabulary, everyday phrases, pronunciation, and simple grammar structures through interactive activities, conversation practice, and cultural activities. No prior foreign language experience is required; ages 16+. Every Thursday, April 2 - May 28 NOR1A: 5-6:30pm - NOR1A is for absolute beginners with no prior language experience NOR1C: 6:45-8pm - NOR1C is for students who may know some basic Norwegian and want to grow their skills

item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Location: Armoire, 4786 1st Avenue South #105, Seattle, WA

Let's get our brains together and make connections!

item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Location: Seattle Public Library - Central Library, 1000 4th Avenue, Seattle, WA

Susan Jones sheds light on her work to decarbonize our built-environment, on mass timber, and our connection to the forests.

item.title: Easy Metal Etching
item.description:
Friday, April 10, 2026 at 11:30 AM

Location: NW Rockhounds, 2720 NE 115th Suite A, Seattle, WA

In this fun etching class, students will learn several uncomplicated ways to transfer a hand drawn or stamped image onto copper or brass.

Event details

item.title: Screen Printing Open Session
item.description:
Friday, April 10, 2026 at 2 PM

Location: Book-It Repertory Theatre, 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, WA

You MUST take the SP 101: Intro to Vera's Screen Print Studio class before you can sign up for an Open Session\\\*\*\* Vera’s screen print studio offers four-hour Open Sessions where individuals who have completed the introductory class may use our screen print lab to work on their own projects. Our Open Sessions are looked over by a volunteer moderator, who can provide you with advice and guidance for anything taught in our Screen Printing 101 class. We offer three different kinds of tickets at Open Sessions: Vacuum Table for paper and other flat media, T-shirt Press for shirts, tote bags and other fabrics, and Screen prep for just screen preparation. Each ticket is limited to two. If the ticket you are looking for is sold out, please add yourself to the waitlist so you can be notified when a ticket becomes available. For Open Sessions, Vera provides: - Rentable screens - Emulsion and emulsion remover - Squegees - Basic tools like spatulas, rulers, tape, etc. Participants must provide their own: - Substrate \(paper, t-shirts, etc\) - Water based ink Vera \*does not\* allow the use of non-water based inks at our studio, as our studio is not set up to safely process non-water based ink. If you have any questions, please email matt@theveraproject.org. \*\*FAQ\*\* 1. Can Vera print a transparent stencil for me? Yes — You must send us your stencil at least 24 hours before the start of your session. Your file must be a .PDF, no larger than 12" x 18", and ready to print. Vera cannot make any edits to your file. Stencils are $5 each and can be paid via Debit/Credit when you arrive. If you are not interested in getting a stencil printed at Vera, you can print your own on transparency at a FedEx or similar print service location. 2. What if I need direct assistance on my projects? Our moderators are trained to assist with any topics covered in the SP 101 class offered at Vera. While some may be able to give you basic advice on your project, they are not expected to ask as a direct instructor. If you have a project where you would like one-on-one guidance, please reach out to matt@theveraproject.org to request private tutoring. 3. I really want to use your studio during an Open Session, but there are no slots available? Our studio is in high demand, so we apologize if our Open Session is sold out — We have limited space in the studio and several events every day at Vera, so it can be difficult for us to find more time for Open Sessions. If you're interested in booking the studio outside of Open Sessions, please email matt@theveraproject.org to discuss private rental rates and availability.

Event details

item.title: Supporting Queer & Trans People in Washington State with Identification Updates
item.description:
Friday, April 10, 2026 at 3:30 PM

A free virtual workshop for social work students interested in supporting queer and trans people in Washington State with identification updates. Workshop provided by the Seattle University Social Work Department, in partnership with the UW-Seattle School of Social Work, UW-Tacoma School of Social Work & Criminal Justice, and Pacific Lutheran University Department of Social Work

Event details

item.title: Stillness in Motion: Architectural Long Exposure - Hands-On Workshop
item.description:
Saturday, April 11, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Bellevue Downtown Park, 10278 NE 1st St, Bellevue, WA

Join a small group of photographers for a hands-on workshop with Sony Artisan Thibault Roland.

Event details

item.title: Homebuyer Education Workshop
item.description:
Saturday, April 11, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: ULMS, 105 14th Avenue, Suite 200, Seattle, WA

This is a free five-hour workshop to help you meet the WA State Housing Finance Commission’s education requirements for down payment assistance and a variety of affordable housing programs and opportunities for qualified applicants! These requirements include the successful completion of a Homebuyers Education Seminar meeting the current FHA, Fannie Mae, FreddieMac, and SMBA benchmark standards.  At the conclusion of the workshop, participants will receive a certificate of achievement from the WA State Housing Finance Commission and DFI valid for two years from the date of issuance.  To join our next workshop, please complete and submit the following registration form at least 24-hours before your selected workshop date. Once this form has been submitted, be sure to check your email for a digital confirmation, as well as additional follow up and important information from a ULMS Housing Department staff member.   In-person workshops are held every 1st and 2nd Saturday of the month from 10:00AM – 3:30PM. They will be held at our Main Office at the following address: 105 14th Ave, Suite 200 Seattle, WA 98122.

Event details

item.title: Master Gardener Plant Clinic
item.description:
Saturday, April 11, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Bellevue Botanical Garden, 12001 Main St, Bellevue, WA

Master Gardeners will be at the Garden to answer your questions on plant selection, maintenance, and general gardening, as well as strategies for managing pest and disease problems. Volunteers provide answers, diagnoses, and helpful science-based information and resources so you can manage your home landscapes with the most sustainable methods. Feel free to bring a bagged sample or photograph for them to review.

Event details

item.title: Book Swap & Sip + Mobile Bookstore
item.description:
Saturday, April 11, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Peace of Mind Brewing, 18411 Highway 99, Lynnwood, WA

Shop Lost The Plot's mobile bookstore while swapping books, mingling with fellow book lovers, and sipping delicious local brews!

Event details

item.title: 3XBA Spring 3x3 Clinic Series
item.description:
Sunday, April 12, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: The Bush School, 3400 E Harrison St, Seattle, WA

Neighborhoods: Capitol Hill,Central Area/Squire Park,First Hill,Judkins Park,Madison Park,Madrona/Leschi,Montlake/Portage BayEvent Types: Community,Education,Hobbies,Holiday/Seasonal Events, Sports/Recreation,Youth Audience: Children,Special Needs,Teen S ponsoring Organization: 3x3 Basketball Association \(3XBA\) Contact: Lakin Roland Contact Email: info@3xba.com Pre-Register: Yes Cost: $250 \+ fees Registration Details: Two sessions offered each Sunday: Grades 2-5 from 9:00 AM to 11:00 AM, Grades 6-8 from 11:00 AM to 1:00 PM. The clinic runs for five Sundays: March 22, March 29, April 5, Apr il 26, and May 3, 2026 at The Bush School, Seattle. Early bird pricin g ends on March 1. Learn more about 3xBA and register at: https://www.3xba.com/register Link: https://3xba.ludus.com/index.php?show\_id=200517791 Help your athlete grow their game this spring with the 3xBA Spring 3x3 Clinic Series-a 5-week program combining fun, competit ive 3x3 basketball with small-group instruction led by 3xBA coaches. Players develop skills, confidence, and basketball IQ through structured play and hands-on coaching. Open to all experience levels for 2nd-8th graders. Athletes should bring a basketball and water bottle. Perfe ct for off-season development and team-style competition.

Event details

item.title: Books & Barre
item.description:
Sunday, April 12, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: Beguiled Books, 109 1st Ave S, Seattle, WA

Join us for a morning Pure Barre pop-up class at Beguiled Books, followed by a private shopping experience before the store opens!

Event details

item.title: Dragon's Landing Celebration and Treasure Hunt!
item.description:
Sunday, April 12, 2026 at 10 AM

Get ready to celebrate Renton’s rooftop dragon at the 7th Annual Dragon’s Landing Street Fair and Dragon Egg Treasure Hunt! 1️⃣ Dragon Egg Scavenger Hunt Starting April 10, follow the clues, find hidden dragon eggs throughout the city, and log your discoveries for a chance to win amazing dragon treasure! 2️⃣ Student Poster Contest K–12 students are invited to design the official poster for the 7th Annual Dragon’s Landing Event. Winners will have their artwork featured at the event, in the student art show, and online! Submissions due March 6 3️⃣ Window Decorating Contest for Businesses Local businesses are invited to decorate their windows in dragon-inspired creativity! Show off your storefront, engage the community, and add to the festive magic of downtown Renton. ✨ Event Day – Sunday, April 12, 2026 Enjoy captivating performances, creative crafts, and fun for all ages as we come together to celebrate our rooftop dragon! Sign up for treasure hunt updates here: https://forms.gle/Tt9g8KUmmBPthmgV6 More information available at Rentonwa.gov/dragonslanding \(check back for updates!\) Step into a world of enchantment and let your imagination soar!

Event details

item.title: Edible Gardening: Pollinators, Pests, and Beneficial Insects
item.description:
Sunday, April 12, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Shoreline Tool Library, 16610 Aurora Avenue North, Shoreline, WA

This class will teach you how to attract beneficial pollinators, deter pests, and identify different critters in your garden.

Event details

item.title: Meditation, Breathwork, Cold 3rd Sunday of the month at Origins Unity
item.description:
Sunday, April 12, 2026 at 11:30 AM

Location: Origins Unity LLC, 229 NW 56th Street, Seattle, WA

Meditation, Breathwork, Cold Plunge & Community! Guided session w/ Certified Yoga, QiGong,Trauma Sensitive, Meditation & Breathwork Teacher

Event details

item.title: Spy Maters Camp II
item.description:
Monday, April 13, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: Suncatcher Forest School, 18701 se 60th st, Issaquah, WA

Calling all young secret agents! This time kids are welcome to join action-packed Outdoor Spy Camp, where kids will train to become top-notch spies through thrilling challenges, daring missions, and teamwork! Obstacle Courses & Ninja Training – Agents-in-training will test their agility, speed, and stealth as they navigate exciting ninja courses, crawl through the forest and learn to use nature objects to complete the challenge. Scavenger Hunts & Riddle Solving – Spies must sharpen their detective skills by decoding secret messages, solving riddles, and uncovering hidden clues to complete their top-secret missions. Nerf Battles & Tactical Challenges – Gear up for friendly Nerf wars, where teamwork and strategy are key to outsmarting the opposition. Agents will also practice their precision and accuracy with target practice and stealth maneuvers. Escape Room-Style Puzzles – Secret agents will work together to crack codes, unlock treasure chests, and solve mind-bending puzzles before time runs out! Outdoor Adventure & Camouflage Training – Spies will blend into their surroundings, practice silent movement, and use nature to their advantage while completing their missions. This high-energy camp is perfect for kids who love adventure, problem-solving, and action-packed fun in the great outdoors! Are you ready to accept the mission? - follow the link to book a spot!

Event details

item.title: Marketing Mondays
item.description:
Monday, April 13, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: Collective Chemistry, 5201 11th Avenue Northwest, Seattle, WA

Sip and strategize in solidarity!

Event details

item.title: EMBODIED ALTARS DANCE CLASS
item.description:
Monday, April 13, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way, Seattle, WA

EMBODIED ALTARS is an all-levels dance class hosted by Daybreak Star Radio. Designed by Indigenous Chicana artist and El Sueño director, Alicia Mullikin. Dancers will utilize Indigenous principles within movement, meditation, and song as a way to practice gratitude, honor the land, and recognize their ancestors. The class is for those who are interested in reflection, intention setting, and a discovery of their own stories. No dance experience necessary. Class Time: Mondays, 6:30-8PM No class 9/15/25 No class 10/13/25 Venue: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way, Seattle, WA 98199 Sliding Scale: $10-$20\* per class. Sign Up Now!   This venue is ADA accessible w/ ADA bathrooms and a wheelchair lift to the second floor. Class will be held in the ballroom space, this area has 5 ADA parking spaces. There are an additional 25 parking spots in our lot, there is ample parking space along the road leading up to Daybreak star as well as the Discovery Park North Parking lot. What to bring: water, comfortable clothes to dance in, journal, your personal identity, a desire to explore new pathways and an open mind/heart. Hosted at Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center Produced by United Indians of All Tribes Foundation In Partnership with El Sueno Dance & Daybreak Star Radio “Indigenize the Airwaves” “Art. Culture. Reclamation.” \*Talk to us about BIPOC and Queer/LGBTQIA2S\+ Scholorships\* Please contact @el.sueno.dance on IG for private conversations about this topic.

Event details

item.title: "Stop the Bleed" class
item.description:
Monday, April 13, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Green Lake Community Center, 7201 East Green Lake Dr N, Seattle, WA

Neighborhoods: Green Lake,Greenwood/Phinney Ridge,Ravenna/ Bryant/Roosevelt Contact: Ann Forrest, OEM Volunteer Contact Email: OEM\_StopTheBleed@Seattle.gov Audience: Adults,Senior,Special Nee ds,Teen Pre-Register: Yes Cost: Free Link: https://www.seattle.gov/emergency-management/community-engagement/stop-the-bleed Life-threatening bleeding injuries can happen anytime. When they do, every minute counts! Stop the Bleed empowers participants to take action! Our trained instructors teach the community about bleeding control: recognizing life-threatening bleeding, applying pressure to the wound, and us ing a tourniquet.

Event details

item.title: Gabor Maté
item.description:
Monday, April 13, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: McCaw Hall, 321 Mercer Street, Seattle, WA

Embark on a profound journey into the intricate landscapes of the human mind and soul with renowned physician, speaker, and bestselling author, Dr. Gabor Maté. With compassion and unwavering authenticity, Dr. Maté explores the interconnectedness of mind, body, and spirit, shedding light on the underlying causes of human suffering and resilience. Drawing from his groundbreaking research on addiction, trauma, and the impact of early childhood experiences, he offers a transformative perspective on healing and personal growth. Through compelling anecdotes, scientific research, and practical wisdom, Dr. Maté invites audiences to reconsider their understanding of mental health, addiction, and the nature of human connection. Whether you're a healthcare professional, a mental health advocate, or simply a curious seeker of truth, this event promises to inspire, educate, and ignite a deeper understanding of the human condition.

Event details

item.title: Global Soccer Game Webinar
item.description:
Tuesday, April 14, 2026 at 2:30 PM

Location: Virtually via zoom, This event will be recorded and distributed after the event to all those who register., Tukwila, WA

Join Us for the FIFA Ready Webinar Date: February 10th, 2:30 PM Location: Virtually Via Zoom Event Overview: As we gear up for the monumental FIFA events, it&'s crucial to align our regional efforts to maximize economic and infrastructural benefits. This meeting will gather key stakeholders from various sectors, including our cities and business community, to strategize and synchronize our readiness plans with an emphasis in preparing our business community to ensure you are ready for this economic opportunity. So do you want to learn about what resources are available to you to maximize this time? Register to learn more now. These quarterly events are brought to you by the Seattle Southside Chamber of Commerce and Explore Seattle Southside. This meeting is a pivotal opportunity for stakeholders to collaborate and ensure our region is fully prepared to host FIFA events successfully. Together, we can create a unified and effective execution plan that benefits all.

Event details

item.title: Hill Talk: Stoup Brewing Capitol Hill
item.description:
Tuesday, April 14, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: Stoup Brewing, 1158 Broadway, Seattle, WA

Description HillTalk at Stoup Brewing \(Capitol Hill\) Tuesday, April 14  4:00-6:00 PM Join GSBA for our next HillTalk as we gather at Stoup Brewing's Capitol Hill taproom for a candid conversation about what's happening in our neighborhood and what it means for local businesses. HillTalk brings together GSBA members, business owners, community partners, and neighborhood leaders for real dialogue and practical updates. Whether you're navigating public safety concerns, city policy changes, foot traffic shifts, or new opportunities for collaboration, this is your space to stay informed and connected. Location: Stoup Brewing - Capitol Hill What is HillTalk? HillTalk is a casual, community-centered gathering where Capitol Hill businesses and community members can hear timely updates, ask questions, share perspective, and connect with the people shaping our neighborhood. What to Expect Neighborhood updates impacting Capitol Hill businesses Connection with fellow business owners and community partners A welcoming, relaxed setting to build relationships and stay engaged Come As You Are Grab a pint, pull up a chair, and be part of the conversation shaping Capitol Hill's future.

Event details

item.title: Utility Board Regular Hybrid Meeting
item.description:
Tuesday, April 14, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: Mercer Island Community & Event Center, 8236 Southeast 24th Street, Mercer Island, WA

Event details

item.title: Type Design Basics
item.description:
Tuesday, April 14, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Seattle Central College Continuing Education, 1701 Broadway, Seattle, WA

Class Overview So you want to design a typeface? Learn how to hand-draw letters and digitize them to create your own unique functional font! In this practical workshop, students will create an original text typeface based on traditional European calligraphy. Whether you want to develop your typography skills, improve your lettering practice, or become a type designer, learning the fundamentals of type design can bring your skills to the next level. Explore both analog media \(pen on paper\) and digital media \(computer\). Learn how to use a calligraphy pen to develop an understanding of the underlying structure of the Latin alphabet. Then you can work on improving your drawings digitally, and create a typeface using Glyphs \(or the font editor of your choice\).   Class time is dedicated to working on your project with individualized feedback, supplemented by lectures, technical demonstrations, and group critique. By the end of the class, you will have a working text typeface of your own creation, as well as a sketch-to-screen workflow that you can use for future font creation. Students will be encouraged to spend up to 10 hours on homework outside of class time. Designing a typeface is a laborious process, but time flies when experimenting, drawing, and editing! This class is offered in partnership with the amazing Seattle Creative Academy \(but this class is not part of their program and is not for credit/degree\)! See also: Experimental Lettering and other Graphic Design or art classes on our website. Learning Objectives Learn the basics of calligraphy Understand the structure and patterning of the Latin alphabet Create your own original letterforms Digitize and refine your original design Learn how to optimize a typeface for text Become familiar with a sketch-to-screen digital type design workflow Who Might Enjoy This Class? Anyone who wants to develop their own custom font Graphic designers, illustrators, lettering artists, type designers -- anyone with related experience who would like to hone their type design skills Architects, engineers, artists, crafters, writers, book lovers -- anyone interested in the form of letters who would enjoy creating and using their own handmade font No prerequisites, but basic graphic design/typography knowledge is recommended

Event details

Tuesday, April 14, 2026 at 6 PM

Event details

item.title: Write a Feature Film
item.description:
Tuesday, April 14, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Seattle Central College Continuing Education, 1701 Broadway, Seattle, WA

Class Overview Do you want to write a screenplay for a feature film, but don’t know where to start? Perhaps you’re a beginner curious about the mechanics of screenplays, or a longtime writer feeling a bit stuck, or a director who would enjoy a new perspective. In this class, gain the skills to turn your ideas into a solid screenplay for a feature film. Gain the tools and building blocks to help guide each part of the writing process for a feature-length screenplay. Learn how to be successful with each step, from idea generation, to writing and revising, and especially outlining and structuring your film. Each 90-minute class will include one or more short in-class exercises, from brainstorming to writing and revising scenes. In the sixth and final class, you will have an opportunity to read an excerpt of your script or present a detailed synopsis to classmates. You’ll be encouraged to work between classes. We’ll look at ways to organize your ideas into an outline that can advance progressively closer to a feature film, discussing diverse and alternative approaches in your structure that might help your script connect with audiences without using clichés. Explore how three-dimensional characters drive plot and how setting, staging, and dialogue can be used to tell your story in an energetic, immersive way. We’ll discuss the differences between writing a screenplay you plan to direct yourself versus one you’re writing for someone else.  There will be handouts and/or reading suggestions offered in class. We’ll look at excerpts from books such as Aristotle’s Poetics and Susan Sontag’s Against Interpretation, as well as film clips and interviews with a diverse array of famous filmmakers across time and space. Get support and guidance on how to find inspiration for your own personal artistic expression and the drive to finish your project. Explore the many joys that can be found in this unique, creative, and multifaceted art form. Learning Objectives Brainstorm and organize ideas for a screenplay Approach structure in a flexible way that allows for different types of films Create three-dimensional characters that form the basis of a story How form can be progressive and new, just like content Find your voice and learn to write comfortably and confidently as yourself Who Might Enjoy This Class? Writers interested in exploring how they might approach a screenplay Anyone who wants to develop their voice as a screenwriter and filmmaker People who have begun a feature-length screenplay and would like the support to continue Artists drawn to other parts of the filmmaking process who would like to explore the writing side Anyone interested in how films are structured and what screenplays are made of Filmmakers who want to create an unconventional piece without sacrificing character and structure No experience is needed; everyone is welcome!

Event details
